author: Maury Vidal
body: Espanyol is selection before it is theatre. The real question is who can start, who only needs an hour, and which attacking combinations should reach Tuesday with legs still under them.

The first leg did not prove Barcelona cannot have the ball; it proved the ball still has to turn into punch. Possession, shots, and corners are not the problem until the decisive pass keeps arriving late.

The Atlético split-screen matters because Barcelona have already seen both versions of the tie: one where the game keeps reopening and one where leverage collapses quickly. The return is not a mystery, but it will punish the wrong first goal.

Femení already has Bayern on the horizon. Even with thinner corroboration around the squad than the desk would like, the present-tense signal is clear: the semifinal is the next real benchmark and the Bonmatí conversation sits inside it.
The clean weekly line is simple: protect the legs, restore the attack, keep the tie football-sized, and let both senior sides reach their next big night with something sturdier than mood.
conviction: The week needs cleaner decisions than louder rhetoric.
dek: Selection, punch, game state, Femení context, and the line of argument that still survives the noise around Espanyol and Madrid.
excerpt: Barcelona's next five days ask for cleaner decisions than louder rhetoric: selection, threat, leverage, Femení pulse, and the weekly line that still holds.
headline: Five Points Before Espanyol and Madrid
heroCaption: Five points. One live argument. No placeholder quiet.
